tools.name: ts.apr.create
tools.summary: Record an approval on an entity.
tools.description:
Records an approval decision on an entity (task, demand, endeavour,
artifact). Approvals are immutable -- once created, they cannot be
modified or deleted. This provides an auditable sign-off trail.

Three verdict types are supported:
- approved: work meets requirements
- rejected: work does not meet requirements
- needs_work: work requires changes before approval

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Entity type: task, demand, endeavour, artifact

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: ID of the entity being approved

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: Role under which approval is given (e.g., reviewer, product_owner)

parameters.name: comment
parameters.example: All acceptance criteria met. Good to ship.

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs (e.g., checklist results, linked artifacts)

example.comment: All acceptance criteria met. Good to ship.

errors.description: No active login for this session
entity_type, entity_id, or verdict missing
Invalid verdict value
Target entity does not exist

examples.title: Approve a task
examples.description: Record an approval decision on a task.

input.comment: All acceptance criteria met. Good to ship.

examples.title: Request changes
examples.description: Record a needs_work verdict with feedback.

input.comment: Missing test coverage for edge cases.

tools.name: ts.apr.get
tools.summary: Retrieve an approval by ID.
tools.description:
Fetches a single approval record by ID. Returns the full approval
including verdict, role, comment, and metadata.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Approval ID is required
Approval with this ID does not exist

tools.name: ts.apr.list
tools.summary: List approvals for an entity.
tools.description:
Lists approval records for a specific entity, newest first.
Supports filtering by approver, verdict, and role.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Entity type: task, demand, endeavour, artifact

parameters.name: verdict
parameters.description: Filter by verdict: approved, rejected, needs_work

returns.description: Returns a paginated list of approvals.

errors.description: No active login for this session
entity_type or entity_id missing

examples.title: List task approvals
examples.description: Get all approvals for a task.

tools.name: ts.art.create
tools.summary: Create a new artifact (reference to external doc, repo, dashboard, etc.).
tools.description:
Creates a new artifact in Taskschmiede.

An artifact is a reference to an external resource -- a document, repository,
link, dashboard, runbook, or any other material relevant to a project. Artifacts
can be linked to an endeavour and/or a specific task.

Artifact kinds: link, doc, repo, file, dataset, dashboard, runbook, other.
Artifacts start in "active" status.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Artifact kind: link, doc, repo, file, dataset, dashboard, runbook, other

parameters.name: summary
parameters.example: Documents the decision to migrate from hard-coded foreign keys to FRM.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Kind and title are required

examples.title: Create a doc artifact
examples.description: Register an architecture decision record linked to an endeavour.

input.summary: Documents the decision to migrate from hard-coded foreign keys to FRM.
input.title: Architecture Decision Record: FRM Migration

examples.title: Create a repo artifact
examples.description: Track a repository as an artifact.

tools.name: ts.art.delete
tools.summary: Delete an artifact (logical delete, sets status to deleted).
tools.description:
Performs a logical delete by setting the artifact's status to 'deleted'.
The artifact is excluded from list results by default but remains in the
database for audit purposes.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Artifact not found

tools.name: ts.art.get
tools.summary: Retrieve an artifact by ID.
tools.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific artifact, including
its kind, URL, tags, and any linked endeavour or task.

example.summary: Documents the decision to migrate from hard-coded foreign keys to FRM.
example.title: Architecture Decision Record: FRM Migration

errors.description: No active login for this session
Artifact with this ID does not exist

tools.name: ts.art.list
tools.summary: Query artifacts with filters.
tools.description:
Lists artifacts with optional filtering and pagination.

Filters can be combined: for example, list all active doc artifacts in an
endeavour with a specific tag. Text search matches against title and summary.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Filter by kind: link, doc, repo, file, dataset, dashboard, runbook, other

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Filter by tag. Single string value; matches artifacts whose tags array contains this substring.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search in title and summary (partial match)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination)

returns.description: Returns a paginated list of artifacts.

errors.description: No active login for this session

examples.description: Find all doc artifacts linked to an endeavour.

examples.description: Find artifacts with a specific tag.

tools.name: ts.art.update
tools.summary: Update artifact attributes (partial update).
tools.description:
Updates one or more fields of an existing artifact. Only provided fields
are changed; omitted fields remain unchanged.

To archive an artifact, set status to "archived". To unlink from an endeavour
or task, pass an empty string.

returns.description: Returns the artifact ID and list of fields that were updated.

Returns audit log entries filtered by entity. Admins can query any entity;

tools.name: ts.audit.list
tools.summary: Query audit logs with filters (admin-only).
tools.description:
Lists audit log entries with optional filtering by action, actor, resource,
IP address, and time range. Admin-only.

Use cursor-based pagination (before_id) instead of offset for stable
results, since the audit middleware logs every request including
pagination queries themselves.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Filter by action (e.g., login_success, login_failure, security_alert)

parameters.name: before_id
parameters.description: Cursor: return entries older than this audit entry ID (stable pagination)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset (prefer before_id for stable results)

returns.description: Returns a paginated list of audit log entries.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Admin privileges required

Does not require admin privileges -- users can always view their own activity.

returns.description: Returns a paginated list of the caller's audit entries.

tools.name: ts.auth.forgot_password
tools.summary: Request a password reset code via email.
tools.description:
Initiates the password reset flow. If the email address is associated with
an account, a reset code is sent via email. The response is identical
regardless of whether the account exists (prevents email enumeration).

When email is not configured, the reset code is returned directly in
the response for development convenience.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address of the account to reset

returns.description: Returns a confirmation that the reset was requested.

example.note: If an account exists with that email, a reset code has been sent.

errors.description: Email is required
Too many requests for this email

examples.title: Request password reset
examples.description: Initiate a password reset for an account.

output.note: If an account exists with that email, a reset code has been sent.

tools.name: ts.auth.login
tools.summary: Authenticate with email and password to get an access token.
tools.description:
Authenticates a user with their email and password credentials.
On success, returns an access token that can be used for subsequent API calls.

The token should be included in the Authorization header as a Bearer token:
Authorization: Bearer <token>

Tokens are single-use session tokens by default. For long-lived API tokens,
use ts.tkn.create after authentication.

returns.description: Returns a session token and user information on success.

errors.description: Email and password are required
Too many login attempts
Email or password is incorrect

examples.title: Basic login
examples.description: Authenticate to get a session token.

tools.name: ts.auth.reset_password
tools.summary: Complete a password reset with the emailed code.
tools.description:
Validates the reset code and sets a new password. On success, all
existing sessions and tokens for the user are invalidated.

The new password must meet the same requirements as registration:
12+ characters, at least one uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Reset code from the email (format: xxx-xxx-xxx)

parameters.name: new_password
parameters.description: New password (12+ chars, mixed case, digit, special)

returns.description: Returns confirmation that the password was reset.

example.note: Password has been changed. All existing sessions have been invalidated.

errors.description: Email, code, and new_password are required
Password does not meet requirements
Invalid or expired reset code

examples.title: Complete password reset
examples.description: Use the emailed code to set a new password.

output.note: Password has been changed. All existing sessions have been invalidated.

tools.name: ts.auth.update_profile
tools.summary: Update your own profile fields.
tools.description:
Allows authenticated users to update their own profile. Only modifies
the caller's own user record -- cannot update other users. Use ts.usr.update
for admin-level user modifications.

errors.description: No active login for this session
At least one field must be provided

returns.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile and limits.

errors.description: No active login for this session

tools.name: ts.cmt.create
tools.summary: Add a comment to an entity.
tools.description:
Creates a comment on any commentable entity (task, demand, endeavour,
artifact, ritual, organization). Comments support Markdown content and
threaded replies via reply_to_id.

The comment author is automatically set to the authenticated user's
resource ID.

parameters.description: Entity type: task, demand, endeavour, artifact, ritual, organization

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: ID of the entity to comment on

parameters.name: content

parameters.name: reply_to_id
parameters.description: Comment ID to reply to (optional, for threaded replies)

tools.name: ts.dmd.cancel
tools.summary: Cancel a demand with a reason.
tools.description:
Convenience tool to cancel a demand. Equivalent to calling ts.dmd.update
with status "canceled" and a canceled_reason, but with a simpler interface.

Both id and reason are required.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.example: No longer needed after scope change

returns.description: Returns the demand ID and list of fields that were updated.

Demand cannot be canceled from its current status

tools.name: ts.dmd.create
tools.summary: Create a new demand (what needs to be fulfilled).
tools.description:
Creates a new demand in Taskschmiede.

A demand represents what needs to be fulfilled -- a feature request, a bug report,
a goal, or any other need. Demands are distinct from tasks: a demand captures the
"what" while tasks capture the "how". A single demand may result in multiple tasks.

Demands start in "open" status and can optionally be linked to an endeavour.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Demand type (e.g., feature, bug, goal, meeting, epic)

parameters.name: description
parameters.example: Users need a dark theme option for reduced eye strain.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Type and title are required

examples.title: Create a feature demand
examples.description: Record a new feature request linked to an endeavour.

input.description: Users need a dark theme option for reduced eye strain.
input.title: Add dark mode support

examples.title: Create a bug demand
examples.description: Report a bug with urgent priority.

tools.name: ts.dmd.get
tools.summary: Retrieve a demand by ID.
tools.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific demand, including
its type, priority, status, and any linked endeavour.

example.description: Users need a dark theme option for reduced eye strain.
example.title: Add dark mode support

errors.description: No active login for this session
Demand with this ID does not exist

tools.summary: Query demands with filters.
tools.description:
Lists demands with optional filtering and pagination.

Filters can be combined: for example, list all high-priority open demands
in an endeavour. Text search matches against title and description.

parameters.description: Filter by status: open, in_progress, fulfilled, canceled

parameters.name: search

tools.name: ts.dmd.update
tools.summary: Update demand attributes (partial update).
tools.description:
Updates one or more fields of an existing demand. Only provided fields
are changed; omitted fields remain unchanged.

Demand status transitions follow a defined lifecycle:
- open -> in_progress, fulfilled, canceled
- in_progress -> fulfilled, canceled
- fulfilled (terminal state)
- canceled (terminal state)

When setting status to canceled, provide a canceled_reason.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: New status: open, in_progress, fulfilled, canceled

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: New priority: low, medium, high, urgent

parameters.name: due_date
parameters.description: Due date (ISO 8601, empty string to clear)

returns.description: Returns the demand ID and list of fields that were updated.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Demand with this ID does not exist
No fields to update or invalid priority value
Invalid status transition

examples.title: Start working on a demand
examples.description: Move a demand from open to in_progress.

examples.title: Fulfill a demand
examples.description: Mark a demand as fulfilled.

tools.description:

guides, and workflows.

Documentation is public and does not require authentication.

returns.description: Returns the document content as Markdown text with YAML frontmatter.

errors.description: name is required

tools.name: ts.dod.assign
tools.summary: Assign a DoD policy to an endeavour.
tools.description:
Assigns a DoD policy to govern task completion within an endeavour.
Each endeavour can have at most one active DoD policy. Assigning a
new policy replaces the previous one.

After assignment, team members should endorse the policy via
ts.dod.endorse to signal agreement.

errors.description: No active login for this session
endeavour_id and policy_id are required
Endeavour or policy does not exist

examples.title: Assign DoD to endeavour
examples.description: Set the DoD policy for an endeavour.

tools.name: ts.dod.check
tools.summary: Evaluate DoD conditions for a task (dry run).
tools.description:
Checks whether a task meets the DoD conditions defined by the policy
assigned to the task's endeavour. This is a dry run -- it does not
modify the task or block status transitions.

Returns the evaluation result for each condition, showing which
conditions pass and which fail, along with the overall verdict.

errors.description: No active login for this session
task_id is required
Task does not exist or has no DoD policy

examples.title: Check task DoD
examples.description: Evaluate whether a task meets its DoD conditions.

tools.name: ts.dod.create
tools.summary: Create a new Definition of Done policy.
tools.description:
Creates a DoD policy with a set of conditions that must be met before
a task can be considered done. Conditions are evaluated automatically
by ts.dod.check.

Each condition has a type (e.g., approval_count, field_set, status_is),
a label, optional parameters, and a required flag. The strictness setting
controls whether all conditions must pass ("all") or a minimum count
("n_of" with quorum).

Four built-in templates are seeded on first run.

parameters.name: description
parameters.example: Requires approval and actual hours logged before marking done.

parameters.name: conditions
parameters.description: Array of condition objects with id, type, label, params, required

parameters.name: quorum
parameters.description: Required count when strictness is n_of

errors.description: No active login for this session
Name or conditions missing

examples.title: Create a DoD policy
examples.description: Define a policy requiring approval and hours logged.

input.description: Requires approval and actual hours logged.
input.name: Standard Task Completion

tools.name: ts.dod.endorse
tools.summary: Endorse the current DoD policy for an endeavour.
tools.description:
Records the caller's endorsement of the DoD policy currently assigned
to an endeavour. Endorsement signals that the team member agrees with
the policy conditions and will follow them.

Endorsements are tracked per policy version. If the policy is
reassigned, previous endorsements are superseded.

errors.description: No active login for this session
endeavour_id is required
Endeavour has no assigned DoD policy
Already endorsed the current policy version

examples.title: Endorse a DoD policy
examples.description: Signal agreement with the assigned DoD policy.

tools.summary: Retrieve a DoD policy by ID.
tools.description:
Fetches a DoD policy including its conditions, strictness settings,
and metadata.

errors.description: No active login for this session
DoD policy ID is required
DoD policy with this ID does not exist

tools.description:
Returns the version lineage of a DoD policy, showing the chain of

errors.description: No active login for this session

Lists DoD policies with optional filtering by status, origin, scope,

tools.name: ts.dod.new_version
tools.summary: Create a new version of a DoD policy with updated conditions.
tools.description:
Creates a new version of an existing DoD policy. The old version is
archived and a predecessor link is established. Existing endorsements
on the old version are superseded -- team members must re-endorse the
new version.

Use this when conditions need to change (conditions cannot be modified
via ts.dod.update). Templates cannot be versioned directly -- fork them
first with ts.dod.create using origin "derived".

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the policy to create a new version of

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the new version (defaults to source name)

parameters.name: conditions
parameters.description: Array of condition objects with id, type, label, params, required

parameters.name: quorum
parameters.description: Required count when strictness is n_of

errors.description: No active login for this session
Policy ID is required
Source policy not found
Cannot version a template policy

examples.title: Update conditions on a policy
examples.description: Create v2 of a policy with an additional peer review condition.

tools.name: ts.dod.override
tools.summary: Override DoD for a specific task (requires reason).
tools.description:
Allows a task to be marked as done even if it does not pass all DoD
conditions. A reason is required and recorded for audit purposes.

Use this sparingly -- overrides bypass governance controls and should
be justified (e.g., hotfix, external dependency, scope change).

parameters.name: reason
parameters.example: Hotfix: customer-blocking issue, approval deferred to post-deploy review.

example.reason: Hotfix: customer-blocking issue, approval deferred to post-deploy review.

errors.description: No active login for this session
task_id and reason are required

examples.title: Override DoD for a hotfix

tools.name: ts.edv.create
tools.summary: Create a new endeavour (container for related work toward a goal).
tools.description:
Creates a new endeavour in Taskschmiede.

An endeavour is a container for related work toward a goal -- similar to a
project, sprint, or epic. Endeavours have optional goals, start/end dates,
and aggregate task progress.

After creation, associate the endeavour with an organization using
ts.org.add_endeavour and add team members with ts.edv.add_member.

tools.name: ts.edv.update
tools.summary: Update endeavour attributes (partial update).
tools.description:
Updates an existing endeavour. Only provided fields are changed;
omitted fields remain unchanged.

Endeavour status transitions follow a defined lifecycle:
- pending -> active, deleted
- active -> on_hold, completed, deleted
- on_hold -> active, deleted
- completed -> archived (completed is terminal; archive to reclaim tier slot)

The deleted status is a soft delete. Deleted endeavours are excluded from
list results by default but can be retrieved by filtering with status=deleted.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: New status: pending, active, on_hold, completed, deleted

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date (ISO 8601, empty string to clear)

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date (ISO 8601, empty string to clear)

returns.description: Returns the list of updated field names.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Endeavour with this ID does not exist
No fields to update
Invalid status transition

tools.name: ts.onboard.health
tools.summary: View agent behavioral health dashboard
tools.description:
Admin-only tool that returns a comprehensive agent behavioral health dashboard.

Includes per-agent success rates (session, 24h rolling, 7d rolling), health status
(healthy, warned, flagged, suspended), and Ablecon traffic light levels (system-wide
and per-organization).

The Ablecon system provides a quick visual indicator of agent behavioral health:
- Level 1 (Green): Normal operations, all metrics within thresholds
- Level 2 (Yellow): Elevated concern, one or more warning conditions active
- Level 3 (Red): Critical, suspension threshold breached or stark deviation detected

returns.description: Returns agent health data, status counts, and Ablecon levels.

tools.name: ts.reg.register
tools.summary: Register a new agent account using an invitation token.
tools.description:
Registers a new agent account using an invitation token.

This is the self-registration endpoint for agents. MCP registration
always creates agent accounts. Human accounts are created through
the web UI. After calling this, the system sends a verification email.
The agent must then call ts.reg.verify with the code from the email
to complete registration.

Password requirements:
- Minimum 12 characters
- At least one uppercase letter
- At least one lowercase letter
- At least one digit
- At least one special character

parameters.name: invitation_token
parameters.description: The invitation token from an admin

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address for the new account

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for the account (must meet requirements)

returns.description: Returns registration status. A verification email is sent.

errors.description: Invitation token is invalid, expired, or exhausted
An account with this email already exists
Email format is invalid
Password does not meet requirements

examples.title: Register with invitation token
examples.description: Agent registers itself using the token provided by admin.

tools.name: ts.reg.resend
tools.summary: Resend the verification email.
tools.description:
Requests a new verification code if the original expired or was lost.

The previous code is invalidated and a new one is sent. The new code
expires in 15 minutes.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to resend verification to

returns.description: Returns confirmation that a new code was sent.

errors.description: Email is required

examples.title: Resend verification code

tools.name: ts.reg.verify
tools.summary: Verify email with the code from the verification email.
tools.description:
Completes registration by verifying the email address.

After calling ts.reg.register, the agent receives an email with a
verification code in the format xxx-xxx-xxx (lowercase alphanumeric).
Submit that code here to complete registration.

On success, returns an auth token that can be used immediately.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Verification code from the email (format: xxx-xxx-xxx)

returns.description: Returns auth token on successful verification.

errors.description: Verification code is incorrect
Verification code has expired
No pending verification for this email

examples.title: Verify email
examples.description: Submit the verification code received via email.

tools.name: ts.rel.create
tools.summary: Create a relationship between two entities.
tools.description:
Creates a typed, directed relationship between any two entities in Taskschmiede.

This is the core of the Flexible Relationship Model (FRM). Instead of hard-coded
foreign keys, entities are connected through generic relations. Common relationship
types include:
- belongs_to: task belongs_to endeavour
- assigned_to: task assigned_to resource
- has_member: organization has_member resource
- governs: ritual governs endeavour
- uses: task uses artifact

Entity types include: task, endeavour, organization, resource, user, demand, artifact, ritual, ritual_run.

Relations can carry metadata (e.g., a role on a membership relation).

parameters.name: relationship_type
parameters.description: Relationship type (e.g., belongs_to, assigned_to, has_member, governs, uses)

parameters.name: source_entity_type
parameters.description: Source entity type (e.g., task, organization, user, ritual)

parameters.name: target_entity_type
parameters.description: Target entity type (e.g., endeavour, resource, artifact)

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.description: Optional metadata on the relationship (e.g., role)

errors.description: No active login for this session
All five required fields must be provided

examples.title: Link a ritual to an endeavour
examples.description: Create a governs relation so the ritual applies to the endeavour.

examples.title: Link a task to an artifact
examples.description: Record that a task uses an artifact.

tools.summary: Remove a relationship.
tools.description:
Deletes a relationship by its ID. This is a hard delete -- the
relationship is permanently removed.

Use ts.rel.list to find the relation ID before deleting.

Creates a new resource in Taskschmiede.

Resources represent work capacity -- the humans, AI agents, services, or
budgets that can be assigned to tasks. Each resource has a type, optional
capacity model, skills, and metadata.

Resource types:
- human: a person
- agent: an AI agent or automated system
- service: an external service or API

After creation, add the resource to an organization with ts.org.add_resource.

tools.name: ts.rtl.create
tools.summary: Create a new ritual (stored methodology prompt).
tools.description:
Creates a new ritual in Taskschmiede.

A ritual is a stored methodology prompt -- the core of BYOM (Bring Your Own
Methodology). Rituals define recurring processes like standups, sprint planning,
retrospectives, or any methodology-specific ceremony.

The prompt field contains the methodology instructions in free-form text. This is
what gets executed during a ritual run. Rituals can be linked to an endeavour via
a governs relationship and can have a schedule (informational only -- the agent
or orchestrator is responsible for triggering runs).

Rituals have an origin field: "custom" for user-created, "template" for built-in
templates, "fork" for rituals derived from another.

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: The methodology prompt (free-form text, BYOM core)
parameters.example: Review yesterday's progress, identify blockers, plan today's work. Focus on tasks in the current endeavour.

parameters.name: description
parameters.example: A brief daily check-in to align the team on progress and blockers.

parameters.name: endeavour_id
parameters.description: Endeavour this ritual governs (creates a governs relation)

errors.description: No active login for this session
Name and prompt are required

examples.title: Create a daily standup ritual
examples.description: Create a ritual for daily standups linked to an endeavour.

input.description: A brief daily check-in to align the team on progress and blockers.
input.name: Daily standup
input.prompt: Review yesterday's progress, identify blockers, plan today's work.

tools.name: ts.rtl.fork
tools.summary: Fork a ritual (create a new ritual derived from an existing one).
tools.description:
Creates a new ritual derived from an existing one. This is how you
evolve methodology prompts while preserving history.

The forked ritual:
- Gets origin="fork" and predecessor_id pointing to the source
- Inherits name, prompt, description, and schedule from the source (unless overridden)
- Is a fully independent ritual that can be modified separately
- Can optionally be linked to a different endeavour

Use ts.rtl.lineage to trace the full version chain.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the fork (defaults to source name)

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.example: Review progress, blockers, and today's plan. Include metrics from the dashboard.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Source ritual does not exist

examples.title: Fork with modified prompt
examples.description: Create a new version of a ritual with an updated prompt.

input.name: Daily standup v2
input.prompt: Review progress, blockers, and today's plan. Include metrics from the dashboard.

examples.title: Fork for a different endeavour
examples.description: Fork a template ritual for a specific project.

tools.name: ts.rtl.get
tools.summary: Retrieve a ritual by ID.
tools.description:
Retrieves detailed information about a specific ritual, including
its prompt, schedule, origin, lineage (predecessor_id), and enabled state.

example.description: A brief daily check-in to align the team.
example.name: Daily standup
example.prompt: Review yesterday's progress, identify blockers, plan today's work.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Ritual with this ID does not exist

output.prompt: Review yesterday's progress, identify blockers, plan today's work.

tools.name: ts.rtl.lineage
tools.summary: Walk the version chain for a ritual (oldest to newest).
tools.description:
Traces the lineage of a ritual by walking the predecessor chain.

Returns an ordered list of rituals from the oldest ancestor to the given
ritual. This shows how a methodology prompt has evolved over time through forks.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Ritual ID to trace lineage from

returns.description: Returns the lineage chain as an ordered list (oldest first).

errors.description: No active login for this session
Ritual with this ID does not exist

examples.title: Trace ritual lineage
examples.description: See the full version history of a forked ritual.

tools.name: ts.rtl.update
tools.summary: Update ritual attributes (cannot change prompt -- fork instead).
tools.description:
Updates one or more fields of an existing ritual. Only provided fields
are changed; omitted fields remain unchanged.

The prompt field cannot be updated directly. To change a ritual's prompt,
use ts.rtl.fork to create a new version with the modified prompt. This
preserves the lineage and audit trail of methodology changes.

returns.description: Returns the ritual ID and list of fields that were updated.

examples.description: Change the schedule for a ritual.

tools.name: ts.rtr.create
tools.summary: Create a ritual run (marks execution start, status=running).
tools.description:
Creates a new ritual run to track execution of a ritual.

A ritual run records when a ritual was executed, by what trigger, and what the
outcome was. New runs start in "running" status with started_at set automatically.

Triggers:
- manual: Agent or human explicitly started the run
- schedule: Triggered by a scheduled event
- api: Triggered by an external API call

tools.name: ts.rtr.update
tools.summary: Update a ritual run (status, results, effects, error).
tools.description:
Updates a ritual run to record its outcome. This is how you complete a run.

Status transitions:
- running -> succeeded (work completed successfully)
- running -> failed (work encountered an error)
- running -> skipped (run was skipped, e.g., nothing to do)

When transitioning to a terminal status, finished_at is set automatically.

The effects field records what the run produced (tasks created, updated, etc.).
The error field captures failure details when status=failed.

example.message: Ritual execution timed out after 5 minutes.

returns.description: Returns the run ID and list of fields that were updated.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Ritual run with this ID does not exist
No fields to update or invalid status transition

examples.title: Complete a successful run
examples.description: Mark a ritual run as succeeded with results.

examples.title: Record a failed run
examples.description: Mark a ritual run as failed with error details.

error.message: Ritual execution timed out after 5 minutes.

tools.name: ts.tkn.create
tools.summary: Create an API access token for a user.
tools.description:
Creates a new API access token for authenticated access.

Unlike session tokens from ts.auth.login, API tokens:
- Can have custom expiration times (or never expire)
- Have a display name for identification
- Are suitable for automation and CI/CD

Only authenticated users can create tokens. By default, tokens are created
for the authenticated user. Admins can create tokens for other users.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: User ID to create token for (defaults to authenticated user)

returns.description: Returns the new token. Store it securely - it cannot be retrieved again.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Cannot create tokens for other users

examples.title: Create a non-expiring token
examples.description: Create an API token that never expires.

examples.title: Create a time-limited token
examples.description: Create a token that expires at a specific time.

tools.name: ts.tkn.verify
tools.summary: Verify a token and return associated user info.
tools.description:
Validates an access token and returns information about the authenticated user.

Use this to:
- Check if a token is still valid
- Get user information for the token holder
- Verify token permissions before performing actions

returns.description: Returns user information if the token is valid.

errors.description: Token parameter is required

examples.title: Verify a token
examples.description: Check if a token is valid and get user info.

Both id and reason are required.

tools.name: ts.tsk.create

tools.description:
Creates a new task in Taskschmiede.

Tasks are the atomic units of work. They can optionally belong to an
endeavour and be assigned to a resource (human or agent). New tasks
start in "planned" status.

parameters.name: description
parameters.example: Implement CRUD tools for the demand entity

tools.name: ts.tsk.list
tools.summary: Query tasks with filters.
tools.description:
Lists tasks with optional filtering and pagination.

Filters can be combined: for example, list all active tasks in an endeavour
assigned to a specific resource. Text search matches against title and description.

Use summary mode (summary: true) to get task counts grouped by status instead of
individual tasks. This is useful for a quick backlog overview.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status: planned, active, done, canceled

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search in title and description (partial match)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination)

parameters.name: summary
parameters.description: If true, return status counts instead of individual tasks

returns.description: Returns a paginated list of tasks, or status counts when summary is true.

errors.description: No active login for this session. Call ts.auth.login first.

examples.title: Summary by status
examples.description: Get a quick overview of task counts grouped by status.

examples.title: List tasks by endeavour
examples.description: Get all tasks belonging to an endeavour.

examples.title: List active tasks assigned to a resource
examples.description: Find tasks currently being worked on by a specific agent.

tools.name: ts.tsk.update
tools.summary: Update task attributes (partial update).
tools.description:
Updates one or more fields of an existing task. Only provided fields
are changed; omitted fields remain unchanged.

Status transitions are validated:
- planned -> active, canceled
- active -> done, canceled, planned
- done -> active (reopen)
- canceled -> planned (reopen)

Lifecycle timestamps are set automatically:
- started_at: set when status changes to active
- completed_at: set when status changes to done
- canceled_at: set when status changes to canceled

To unassign or unlink, pass an empty string for assignee_id or endeavour_id.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: New status: planned, active, done, canceled

parameters.name: assignee_id
parameters.description: New assignee resource (empty string to unassign)

parameters.name: due_date
parameters.description: Due date (ISO 8601, empty string to clear)

parameters.name: canceled_reason
parameters.description: Reason for cancellation (when setting status to canceled)

returns.description: Returns the task ID and list of fields that were updated.

errors.description: No active login for this session
Task with this ID does not exist
Invalid status transition (e.g., planned -> done)

examples.title: Start working on a task
examples.description: Transition a task from planned to active.

examples.title: Complete a task
examples.description: Mark an active task as done.

tools.name: ts.usr.create
tools.summary: Create a new user account.
tools.description:
Creates a new user in the system. There are two paths for user creation:

**Admin creation**: An authenticated admin can directly create users for any organization.

**Self-registration**: A user can register themselves using an organization's registration token.
This requires:
- organization_id: The organization to join
- org_token: A valid registration token for that organization
- password: The user's chosen password

Self-registration is useful for onboarding new team members or agents.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Organization to add user to (required for self-registration)

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: External system ID for SSO integration (optional)

errors.description: No valid authentication and no org_token provided
Organization token is invalid or expired
A user with this email already exists
Email format is invalid
Password does not meet requirements

examples.title: Self-registration with org token
examples.description: Register a new user using an organization's registration token.

examples.title: Admin creates user
examples.description: An authenticated admin creates a user directly.
